summary: This cluster of research focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, integrating molecular phylogenetics with detailed morphological analysis to resolve species boundaries and evolutionary relationships. The studies established that the subgenus *Halamphora* is phylogenetically distinct, supporting its reclassification as a separate genus, and formally described *Amphora terroris* as a new species. A key pattern across the cluster is the reliance on combined molecular and morphological data to clarify the systematic placement of species within this globally distributed group, which inhabits diverse marine and estuarine environments.
